metadid 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles

Summarise simulated DiD data to study-level statistics

Source: R/simulate.R
as_summary_did.Rd

Aggregates individual-level data from simulate_meta_did() to the four-cell summary (pre/post × control/treatment) required by meta_did(). The pre-post correlation \(\rho\) is estimated empirically from matched pairs within each arm and then averaged.

Usage

as_summary_did(sim)

Arguments

sim

A data frame produced by simulate_meta_did().

Value

A data frame with one row per study in the format required by validate_summary_data() for design "did".

Examples

sim <- simulate_meta_did(n_studies = 3, seed = 1)
as_summary_did(sim)
#> # A tibble: 3 × 13
#>   study_id design n_control n_treatment mean_pre_control mean_post_control
#>   <chr>    <chr>      <int>       <int>            <dbl>             <dbl>
#> 1 study_1  did          100         100            0.463             0.438
#> 2 study_2  did          100         100            0.441             0.424
#> 3 study_3  did          100         100            0.452             0.434
#> # ℹ 7 more variables: sd_pre_control <dbl>, sd_post_control <dbl>,
#> #   mean_pre_treatment <dbl>, mean_post_treatment <dbl>,
#> #   sd_pre_treatment <dbl>, sd_post_treatment <dbl>, rho <dbl>
